content.claim: As of June 2026, the newly appointed Education Minister Judit Lannert publicly questioned the admissions system supporting 6- and 8-year gimnazium tracks, arguing research shows selective-track performance gains are primarily explained by family background rather than institutional value-added, and committed to placing admissions reform on the government agenda for social consultation.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Implementation planning lens
limitations: Minister clarified her proposal scope is narrower than opposition framing suggests—examination of admissions system, not abolition of tracks
Statement remained at agenda-setting stage as of source date; no formal reform plan or timeline published
Immediate partisan opposition from Fidesz frakcióvezetője within 24-48 hours created political contestation without resolution pathway
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
